title: Lupinaster alpinus
intro: Lupinaster alpinus, also known as Alpenstrauch, is a plant species belonging to the family Leguminosae, characterized by its shrubby growth habit and delicate, blue-violet flowers. The health benefits of Lupinaster alpinus include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various cardiovascular and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lupinaster alpinus is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in the production of dried flowers and leaves for use in traditional medicine and folk remedies.
taxonomy: Lupinaster alpinus is a plant species that belongs to the family Oleaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the olive family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf shape and fruit type. Overall, the classification of Lupinaster alpinus reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the family.
